Controls.Caption: Any modifications made to the training will only apply to future instances.
Training Name
Controls.Placeholder: Provide a short description of the training

Controls.Caption: Version
Controls.Tooltip:
The minor version number increases when an unapproved training definition is edited.
The major version number increases when an approved training definition is edited.

Controls.Caption: Activation Date
Controls.Tooltip: Trainees will receive their training tasks as of this date - assuming the Status of the training definition is Active.

Controls.Caption: Frequency
Controls.Tooltip: How often the training should be repeated.

Controls.Caption: Length (Hours)
Controls.Tooltip: Estimated length of the training in hours.

Controls.Caption: Deadline (Days)
Controls.Tooltip: How many days the trainee has to complete the training once they receive the task.

Controls.Caption: Status
Controls.Tooltip:
Training tasks are only created if the Status is set to Active.
Use Inactive to create a training definition but not send training tasks to trainees.

Controls.Caption: Source
Controls.Tooltip: Source/origin of the training program.

Controls.Caption: Assessment Method
Controls.Tooltip:
Attendance: No specific assessment method enforced beyond a sign-off by a designated reviewer.
Score: Post-Training assessment requires a passing grade on one of the questionnaires.
Certification: Trainee will receive certification details to enter before submitting their training for review.

Controls.Caption: Training Definition Reviewer
Controls.Tooltip: The training definition reviewer will approve or reject the training definition.

Controls.Caption: Training Execution Reviewer
Controls.Tooltip: The training execution reviewer decides whether each trainee has completed or should retake the training.

Controls.Caption: Trainings consist of up to 4 phases.  Select phases that will apply to this training and then define each phase in the sections that appear below.
Include pre-assessment
Controls.Tooltip: Questionnaire(s) to be answered by the trainee before proceeding to the rest of the training.

Controls.Caption: Include documentation to read
Controls.Tooltip: Documentation for the trainee to read as part of their training.

Controls.Caption: Include training course(s) to execute
Controls.Tooltip:
Training courses for the trainee to execute as part of their training.
These are entered as URLs to courses hosted outside of the training task.

Controls.Caption: Include post-assessment
Controls.Tooltip: Questionnaire(s) to be answered by the trainee after completing all previous sections, meant to gauge effectiveness of the training or gather feedback.

Controls.Caption:
Select which type(s) of questionnaire to use for pre-training assessment.
Any combination of Default, Customized or External questionnaires can be used.
Include a Default Pre-Training Questionnaire
Controls.Tooltip: Pre-defined questions intended to determine each trainee's level of experience with the subject matter.

Controls.Caption: Create a Customized Pre-Training Questionnaire
Controls.Tooltip: Define any number of pre-training questions to be answered by the trainee.

Controls.Caption: Link an External Pre-Training Questionnaire
Controls.Tooltip: Enter a URL that links to an external pre-training questionnaire to be completed by the trainee.

Controls.Caption:
The default pre-training questionnaire consists of these questions:
• Have you been exposed to this topic in the past? (yes/no)
• Have you ever been trained on this topic? (yes/no) 
• Do you have practical experience related to this topic? (yes/no)
• Do you consider yourself a Subject Matter Expert?​ (yes/no)
• Do you feel you know this topic well enough to not require training? (yes/no)
• Rate your current knowledge of the associated training topic (1 to 10)
• Based on your knowledge of this topic, what are the key points that you hope this training will cover in detail? (optional)

Controls.Caption: Select Process(es) to assign, then click Assign Selected

Once assigned, Processes are listed in the grid below.
You can edit any row to specify a version of the Process to use, or mark the Process as supporting documentation.
Supporting documentation is meant to provide the user with additional information, and will not create a training task in EPC.

Controls.Caption:
Select which type(s) of questionnaire to use for post-training assessment.
Any combination of Default, Customized or External questionnaires can be used.
At least one customized post-training question/External Post training questionnaire should be included when the user selects the score.
Include a Default Post-Training Questionnaire
Controls.Tooltip: Pre-defined questions intended to gather feedback from each trainee on the training program.

Controls.Caption: Create a Customized Post-Training Questionnaire
Controls.Tooltip: Define any number of post-training questions for the trainee to answer.

Controls.Caption: Link an External Post-Training Questionnaire
Controls.Tooltip: Enter a URL that links to an external pre-training questionnaire for the trainee to complete.

Controls.Caption:
The default post-training questionnaire consists of these questions:
• Rate your own knowledge of the training topic upon completion of the training. (1 to 10)
• Do you feel the training brought value? (yes/no)
• Do you feel the training was adequate & complete? (yes/no)
• Would you be interested in attending additional trainings related to this topic? (yes/no)
• What did you feel was incorrect or should be updated in the training? (optional)
• What are the key points that you felt should be covered in greater detail? (optional)
• What are the key topics that weren’t covered at all during the training? (optional)
